key_insight: Adaptive Mamba Neural Operators (AMO) replace the usual kernel integral view of state-space models with reproducing-kernel constructions based on Takenaka–Malmquist systems, enabling flexible PDE solution learning on diverse geometries and meshes.
engineering_relevance: AMO is relevant for simulation workflows because it targets accurate surrogate solvers for PDEs across point clouds, structured meshes, grids, and irregular domains.
method: Builds a neural operator around state-space models with reproducing kernels.
Uses Takenaka–Malmquist systems to adaptively represent PDE solution manifolds.
Aligns the operator design with adaptive Fourier decomposition (AFD) theory.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on challenging benchmark PDEs in fluid physics, solid physics, and finance, where AMO consistently achieves lower relative L2 error than state-of-the-art solvers.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the Takenaka–Malmquist/AFD representation is constructed for a given geometry or mesh, which could add implementation complexity.
